sections.heading: Types of states
content: States show the interaction status of a component or UI element and ensure accessibility.

Enabled/Active: An enabled state communicates an interactive component or element.
Hovered: A hovered state communicates when a user has placed a cursor over an interactive element.
Focused: A focused state communicates when a user has highlighted an element using an input method such as a keyboard or voice.
Pressed: A pressed state communicates when a user clicks or taps an element.
Dragged: A dragged state communicates when a user presses and moves an element.
Selected: A selected state communicates when a user has chosen an element using a cursor, keyboard, voice, or tap. This state is typically indicated by a check mark icon, a checkbox component, a change in surface color, or a combination of these indicators.
Disabled: A disabled state communicates an inoperable component or element.

sections.heading: State layers
content: Front-end engineering: We decided to use brightness filters to apply the different state styles more consistently and easily, especially for customizable components. July 17, 2025

sections.heading: Interaction & motion define how elements respond and animate to provide feedback, guide users, and enhance usability.
